title: Purity followed by aliquot titration
problemPrompt: A 1.50 g impure carbonate MCO3 is dissolved in excess acid and the solution is made up to 250.0 cm3. A 25.0 cm3 aliquot requires 20.00 cm3 of 0.100 mol dm−3 NaOH to neutralise the remaining acid. Additional problem data define the initial acid amount and the carbonate reaction. Determine the percentage purity of MCO3.

requiredEvidence.answerRequirement: cannot be completed until initial acid amount and Mr(MCO3) are supplied

steps.expression: verify initial acid amount and molar mass evidence

failureTaxonomy.supportHint: Back titration needs the initial acid amount before the amount consumed can be found.

teacherReview.notes: This is intentionally incomplete and should not invoke a numerical Diagnosis component.
